ASBESTOS LAW AND MOTION 503, DEFENDANT IMO INDUSTRIES INC.'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT. HEARING HELD AND TENTATIVE RULING ADOPTED: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT AND ALTERNATE MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION ARE DENIED. DEFENDANT FAILED TO SUSTAIN ITS INITIAL BURDEN OF DEMONSTRATING THAT PLAINTIFFS DO NOT POSSESS AND CANNOT REASONABLY OBTAIN EVIDENCE SUPPORTING THEIR CLAIMS AGAINST DEFENDANT. DEFENDANT SUBMITTED PLAINTIFFS' RESPONSES TO DEFENDANT'S CONTENTION DISCOVERY THAT WERE UNVERIFIED. WHERE A VERIFICATION IS REQUIRED, AN UNVERIFIED RESPONSE IS EQUIVALENT TO NO RESPONSE AT ALL. (APPLETON V. SUP. CT. (1988) 206 CAL.APP.3D 632, 636.) BY FAILING TO DEMONSTRATE THAT PLAINTIFFS SUBMITTED FACTUALLY DEVOID VERIFIED RESPONSES TO CONTENTION DISCOVERY DESIGNED TO ELICIT ALL THE EVIDENCE PLAINTIFFS HAVE TO SUPPORT THEIR CLAIMS, DEFENDANT IS PRECLUDED FROM SUCCESSFULLY INVOKING THE FACTUALLY DEVOID PRONG OF AGUILAR. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: TERI L. JACKSON, MATTER NOT REPORTED

MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
Off Calendar Apr-05-2012 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES:
Ryan Kujawski, Esq., of HOWARD ROME MARTIN & RIDLEY LLP (650-365-7715, X226), appearing for defendant / moving party Imo Industries Inc.
J. Bruce Jackson, Esq., of KELLER, FISHBACK & JACKSON LLP (415-775-1206), appearing for plaintiff / opposing party Mary Roberta Holland

RULING:
DEFENDANT IMO INDUSTRIES INC.'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT

Hearing held and tentative ruling adopted: Defendant Imo Industries Inc.'s motion for summary judgment and alternate motion for summary adjudication are denied. Defendant failed to sustain its initial burden of demonstrating that plaintiffs do not possess and cannot reasonably obtain evidence supporting their claims against defendant. Defendant submitted plaintiffs' responses to defendant's contention discovery that were unverified. Where a verification is required, an unverified response is equivalent to no response at all. (Appleton v. Sup. Ct. (1988) 206 Cal.App.3d 632, 636.) By failing to demonstrate that plaintiffs submitted factually devoid verified responses to contention discovery designed to elicit all the evidence plaintiffs have to support their claims, defendant is precluded from successfully invoking the factually devoid prong of Aguilar. Order signed in open court. Judge: Teri L. Jackson, Matter not reported (503/TLJ)
